title: ZeroLeaks Architecture Tour
description: Explore the multi-agent AI security scanner that tests LLM systems for prompt injection vulnerabilities using research-backed attack techniques.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of LLM prompt injection

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: ZeroLeaks is an autonomous AI security scanner that tests LLM systems for prompt injection vulnerabilities. It uses a multi-agent architecture with Tree of Attacks (TAP) to systematically explore attack vectors and identify weaknesses in system prompt defenses. The codebase is organized into three main pillars: agents (the brain), probes (the arsenal), and knowledge (the research base).

interactiveActions.description: Click src/index.ts to see all public API exports

steps.title: Multi-Agent Architecture
steps.description:
The heart of ZeroLeaks is its multi-agent system. The Scan Engine orchestrates a team of specialized agents:

Strategist selects attack strategies based on defense analysis. Attacker generates attack prompts using the Tree of Attacks pattern. Evaluator assesses whether attacks extracted sensitive content. Mutator evolves failed attacks into more effective variants. Inspector is a TombRaider-inspired defense fingerprinting agent. Orchestrator coordinates multi-turn attack sequences (Siren, Echo Chamber). Target wraps the LLM being tested. Injection Evaluator is a specialized evaluator for prompt injection tests.

interactiveActions.description: Click engine.ts to see how the scan engine orchestrates all agents

steps.title: Attack Probe Library
steps.description:
Probes are pre-crafted attack prompts organized by technique category. ZeroLeaks ships with 15+ categories of attack probes covering the full spectrum of LLM exploitation:

direct, social, and persona cover classic prompt extraction techniques. encoding covers Base64, ROT13, and Unicode bypass attacks. modern includes Crescendo, Many-Shot, CoT Hijack, and Policy Puppetry. hybrid covers XSS/CSRF-style combined attacks. tool-exploits covers MCP and tool-calling exploitation. garak-inspired contains probes based on NVIDIA's Garak scanner. injection is the full prompt injection test suite.

The probe index aggregates all categories and provides query functions for selecting probes by category, defense level, or attack phase.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.ts to see how all probe categories are aggregated

steps.title: Research Knowledge Base
steps.description:
The knowledge module contains research-backed security intelligence that powers the scanner's effectiveness.

techniques.ts contains documented attack techniques from CVEs and academic papers (EchoLeak, TAP, PAIR, Crescendo, TombRaider, Siren, and more). payloads.ts has proven payload templates for extraction, bypass, context injection, and instruction override. exfiltration.ts covers data exfiltration vectors including image-based, link-based, and encoded response methods. defense-bypass.ts contains methods for bypassing known defense systems (Prompt Shield, Llama Guard, NeMo Guardrails, etc.).

interactiveActions.description: Click techniques.ts to see the documented CVE and research references

steps.title: Type System & Configuration
steps.description:
ZeroLeaks has a rich type system that models the entire security scanning domain. Key types include ScanConfig and ScanResult for core scan configuration and output, AttackNode and AttackStrategy for Tree of Attacks data structures, DefenseProfile and DefenseFingerprint for defense analysis models, MultiTurnSequence for multi-turn attack patterns (Siren, Echo Chamber, TombRaider), and Finding and InjectionTestResult for vulnerability findings.

Configuration files at the root control the build (tsconfig.json), formatting (biome.jsonc), and package definition (package.json).

interactiveActions.description: Click types.ts to explore the full type system

steps.title: Examples & Getting Started
steps.description:
The examples directory contains ready-to-run scripts showing how to use ZeroLeaks.

basic-scan.ts is a simple system prompt scan with default settings. custom-engine.ts shows advanced usage with createScanEngine() for fine-tuned control over tree depth, branching, and attack techniques. probe-library.ts demonstrates querying and using the probe library directly.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the full codebase — you now know the architecture!

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: Here's ZeroLeaks at a glance. The scanner flows from the CLI or programmatic API through the Scan Engine, which coordinates the multi-agent team (Strategist, Attacker, Evaluator, Mutator, Inspector, Orchestrator) against a Target LLM. Agents draw from the Probe Library (15+ attack categories) and the Knowledge Base (CVE techniques, payloads, exfiltration vectors, defense bypasses) to systematically test system prompt security. Results are scored from 'secure' to 'critical' with detailed findings, defense profiles, and actionable recommendations.

interactiveActions.description: Explore freely — you now know the full ZeroLeaks architecture!
